name: Almond Breakfast Round
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small mixing bowl, dissolve yeast in warm water; set aside.
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, whole wheat flour, 1/4 cup brown sugar, salt, and nutmeg (or cardamom). Add cold butter and cut into the mixture until it resembles fine crumbs. Stir in softened yeast, eggs, milk, and almond extract until combined. Cover and refrigerate for 1-2 hours or until dough is easy to handle.
3. For filling: In a bowl, mix ground almonds, 3 tablespoons brown sugar, granulated sugar, and allspice (or cardamom). Set aside.
4. Turn dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Divide into 3 portions. Shape each into a ball. Cover and let rest for 10 minutes.
5. Gently roll out each piece of dough to a 12-inch circle. Place one circle on a greased pizza pan or large baking sheet. Brush with half the melted butter, sprinkle with half the filling. Cover with another circle of dough. Brush remaining butter over top and sprinkle with remaining filling. Top with final dough circle.
6. Using kitchen shears or a sharp knife, cut stack into 12 wedges, cutting to within 1-inch of the center. Gently lift each wedge and turn twice to create a twist. Cover with plastic wrap and chill for 2-24 hours before baking.
7. Let stand at room temperature for 20 minutes before baking. Optionally brush with milk; sprinkle with coarse sugar.
8. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F for 30-35 minutes, or until bread sounds hollow when tapped. Allow to cool on foil briefly on a rack. Serve warm, cutting into wedges and serving on a platter.
